frame_analyses.description: FLS tutorial title screen for Task 5: Intracorporeal Suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Introduction screen - timing not started yet

frame_analyses.description: Trainee preparing instruments outside the laparoscopic trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase - instruments visible but not yet in the field

frame_analyses.description: First view of the task area with Penrose drain and needle driver entering field
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Timing starts - needle driver visible in field with suture

frame_analyses.description: Attempting first suture placement through the Penrose drain at the marked location
Continuing suture placement through the drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady hand control during needle passage

frame_analyses.description: Completing passage through drain and preparing for second mark
Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot (double throw)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand positioning for surgeon's knot initiation

frame_analyses.description: Performing second throw with hand switching
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand alternation from first throw

frame_analyses.description: Executing third and final throw with hand switching
Final knot secured and ready for cutting

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot double throw executed
Correct single throw with proper hand switching
Final throw completed with appropriate tension

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well approximated with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Excellent performance demonstrating proficient technique with proper surgeon's knot formation, correct hand switching sequence, and accurate suture placement. The trainee completed the task efficiently with minimal penalties.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly more precise needle placement at the marks to minimize deviation penalties
Continue practicing to maintain consistency in timing across attempts
strengths: Proper surgeon's knot technique with double throw
Correct hand switching between all throws
